## 0. TL;DR for the picking-up model

You are picking up a static, hand-coded HTML/CSS/JS website for a real local roofing business in coastal North Carolina. The client is **Gustavo Vera** (owner-operator). The phone is `910.228.8034`, email `verasroofing@gmail.com`, service area is **Brunswick + New Hanover County, NC** (Wilmington, Carolina Beach, Leland, Southport, Oak Island, Bald Head Island, Castle Hayne, etc.).

The site is **not broken** but it is **not done**. The visual direction is locked (homepage + `/photos.html` gallery). The next session's job is to:

1. **Find the new EPDM photos from the Carolina Beach job** (Alex says they're in Apple Photos on his iPhone, not yet uploaded anywhere). Without them, you can't add the "Flat roof reference" block Gustavo asked for in his last message.
2. **Fix two small live-site bugs** the June 9 audit found: "Hyperstock" (a fake product name, 3 occurrences in the live HTML) and the stale `sitemap.xml <lastmod>` date.
3. **Resolve the June 4 mobile punch list** (P1) before any more deploy work.
4. **Re-word the "owner inspects the finished work before the job is complete" line** so it doesn't read as a contradiction (Gustavo flagged this on 2026-06-08).

Then re-verify with the browser tool and get explicit approval before any deploy.

**Do not deploy. Do not contact Gustavo. Do not promise dates.** This is a handoff-first, careful-claim-language, no-fabrication project. See the *Guardrails* section.

## 1. The job in one paragraph

Vera's Roofing LLC is a 1-person owner-operator roofing business (Gustavo, does the work himself + 1 crew) in coastal NC. Website lives at `vera-roofing-review-public.vercel.app`. Pure static HTML + one CSS file + one tiny JS file, deployed via Vercel. Designed and built incrementally over the last 2 weeks by Alex (the human you're talking to) with help from Hermes/Grok/Codex. Now in **polish + content-completion** phase.

The visual direction is **the live site today**: dark purple/navy + silver + cream, big Antonio/Manrope typography, real local job photos (no stock), strong "owner-inspected / hand-nailed" trust story, phone-and-text-first CTAs, no forms. Approved logo: `vera-roofing-logo-mainmark-transparent-clean.png` (purple + silver + white, "Logo 10" direction, no utility knife, no circled hammer). Don't go redesigning.

## 4. Gustavo's actual feedback (verbatim, 2026-06-08)

These are the two things he said, captured from your Telegram chat on 2026-06-08 around 07:44 UTC. The two composer images (`composer_2026-06-08_07-44-20-622_ccbbf7.png` and `composer_2026-06-08_07-44-34-037_76d1b1.png` in `AppData\Roaming\Hermes\composer-images\`) are screenshots you sent of the new EPDM photos and possibly the homepage line he was reacting to.

> **Gustavo (1):** *"I'm currently in the middle of doing an EPDM flat roof that we can use pics of for the flat roof reference."*
>
> **Gustavo (2):** *"On the pic where it says 'owner inspects the finished work before the job is complete' seems like we need to word that differently if I inspect the finished work before it is complete seems contradictive. What are your thoughts?"*

### How to handle these

**For (1) — the EPDM photos:**
1. First, **find the originals on this laptop.** See §5 for the search checklist. The current `public/assets/images/roof-epdm-flat-roof-reference.jpg` (367 KB, dated 2026-06-09) appears to be a generic/stock EPDM reference already, not the new Carolina Beach job. You need to confirm.
2. If originals aren't on the laptop yet, **ask Alex how to get them** (AirDrop? iCloud web upload? iPhone USB transfer? Email to self?). Don't guess — Apple Photos on iPhone does not expose the original full-res JPEGs to the filesystem in a way you can read from this Windows laptop without iCloud Photos being set up.
3. Once you have the photos, the plan is to (a) drop the new ones into `public/assets/images/` with names like `roof-epdm-carolina-beach-progress-1.jpg` etc., (b) replace or supplement the EPDM image on the homepage and `/photos.html`, and (c) update the "Low-slope & specialty systems" service card (and the Hyperstock line — see O3) to reference EPDM specifically.

**For (2) — the reword:**
1. **Find every place** in `public/index.html`, `public/photos.html`, root `index.html`, root `photos.html` that contains "owner inspects", "before the job is complete", or "finished work before".
2. **Current live phrasing (read from `public/index.html` and `public/photos.html`):** "Owner hand-inspected" (the section heading) and on the photos page: the "Inspection details" figure caption / section copy. The exact contradiction line Gustavo cited reads like: *"...every finished roof gets inspected by the owner before we call it complete"* (paraphrase — verify the exact line before editing).
3. **Suggested rewrites** (pick one and ask Alex to approve before applying):
   - *"Every finished roof is inspected by the owner before final payment — not by the crew that installed it."*
   - *"Owner-inspected at the wrap-up walkthrough, not when the crew is ready to leave the site."*
   - *"Hand-nailed and owner-inspected at the finish — you don't pay until Gustavo walks it."*
4. **Guardrail:** Don't add claims that aren't true. No "100% of jobs", no "guaranteed", no "every single time" unless you can verify the precise prior pattern. The "owner-inspected" story is real (Gustavo does the inspections himself per the business card and his own words). Phrase it as **what happens**, not as a promise.
5. **Apply to both `public/*` and root mirror.** Back up first to `backups/owner-inspect-reword-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS/`. `cmp -s` must return 0 after.

## 5. Finding the new EPDM photos (the Apple Photos problem)

**Heads up:** this is the part most likely to need human help. Apple Photos on iPhone is its own walled garden — the originals are stored on the phone and (optionally) in iCloud. On a Windows laptop with no iCloud for Windows app installed, **you cannot read the originals from the filesystem**. The composer images from June 8 in `AppData\Roaming\Hermes\composer-images\` are the **screenshots Alex sent into the chat** — they are not the original JPEGs from Gustavo's camera.

### What to do, in order

1. **Confirm what's already on disk.** Search the local filesystem for anything that looks like the EPDM photos:

   ```bash
   cd /c/Users/alexl
   # Common places new iPhone photos land on Windows
   ls -la "Pictures/" 2>/dev/null        # user's Pictures (currently empty/no iCloud photos)
   ls -la "OneDrive/Pictures/" 2>/dev/null
   ls -la "Downloads/" 2>/dev/null
   # Any EPDM / Carolina Beach / recent phone dump?
   search_files pattern="*epdm*"
   search_files pattern="*carolina*beach*"
   search_files pattern="*iphone*"
   search_files pattern="*img_*"   # default iPhone filename pattern
   search_files pattern="*IMG_*"
   search_files path="OneDrive" pattern="*2026-06*"
   search_files path="Pictures" pattern="*2026-06*"
   search_files path="Downloads" pattern="*2026-06*"
   ```

2. **If nothing turns up**, the photos are still on the iPhone. Ask Alex to pick one of these (in order of preference for full quality):
   - **AirDrop to this Windows laptop** (works over Wi-Fi/Bluetooth, no cloud, keeps original JPEGs). Drops into `C:\Users\alexl\Downloads\` or wherever he picks.
   - **iPhone USB transfer** (plug in, "Trust this computer", copy from `Internal Storage\DCIM\`).
   - **Email to self** with the original JPEGs attached (slow for many photos, gets compressed by some mail providers).
   - **iCloud web upload** via icloud.com (works but cumbersome for many photos).
   - **iCloud for Windows** app install + Apple ID sign-in (Alex can do this; lets iCloud Photos sync to a local `Pictures\iCloud Photos\` folder).
   - **Google Drive / Dropbox mobile app upload** (low friction, but the user said Apple Photos specifically, so this would be a workaround).

3. **Once the photos are on disk**, copy them into the project:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p "/c/Users/alexl/Documents/Codex/LarryBuildsAI/local-only/vera-roofing-recovery/deploy-public/vera-roofing-review-public/public/assets/images/incoming-epdm-carolina-beach"
   cp "<source>/IMG_*.JPG" "<dest>/"
   ```
   Suggested filename pattern: `roof-epdm-carolina-beach-01-progress.jpg`, `roof-epdm-carolina-beach-02-membrane.jpg`, etc.

4. **Run the photo rules** before publishing (these came from the VPS hermes skill `client-photo-curation/references/vera-roofing-photo-rules.md` — worth re-loading for the full ruleset):
   - Group by logical job (don't mix Carolina Beach EPDM with anything else in the same visual block).
   - Prefer versions with location/date metadata (phone overlays) when both exist.
   - Keep problem/failure roofs separate from finished work.
   - Add real, descriptive alt + caption that matches what's visibly shown (no "EPDM roof" if it's actually TPO).

5. **Update the site** only after Alex approves the exact image set + placements.

### What's already there for EPDM today
```text
public/assets/images/roof-epdm-flat-roof-reference.jpg   (367 KB, 2026-06-09)
```
This file exists in the live site (used somewhere — verify placement in `public/index.html` and `public/photos.html`). It's labeled "reference" not "carolina-beach" so it's likely a generic EPDM reference, not Gustavo's actual job. Good chance the new photos should **replace** this image with a real "Gustavo on a Carolina Beach job" hero.

## 7. Guardrails (read these before editing)

These come from the original handoff + every prior session. They are not optional.

1. **Do not deploy without explicit approval.** The `vercel deploy --prod --yes --scope orbitals-projects` command is in the handoff. Wait for Alex to say "deploy" or equivalent. No "while I was at it" deploys.
2. **Do not contact Gustavo directly.** All communication goes through Alex.
3. **Careful claim language.** Avoid unless verified:
   - "guaranteed insurance approval"
   - "best roofer" / "top roofer"
   - "BBB accredited"
   - "GAF certified" / "CertainTeed certified" / "Owens Corning preferred"
   - "FORTIFIED certified" (say "FORTIFIED-style planning" instead)
   - "family owned since YEAR" (need proof)
   - "licensed & insured" — only if written proof is on file
   - "50-year warranty" — qualify with which manufacturer + which conditions
4. **Static, handoff-first.** Plain HTML/CSS + tiny JS. No WordPress, no database, no CMS, no build step. Alex wants a site a non-tech person can edit via copy-paste in VS Code.
5. **Source of truth + mirror discipline.** Canonical files live in `public/`. Root `index.html` and root `photos.html` are mirrors. After ANY edit to a public file, mirror to root and verify with:
   ```bash
   cd '/c/Users/alexl/Documents/Codex/LarryBuildsAI/local-only/vera-roofing-recovery/deploy-public/vera-roofing-review-public'
   cmp -s public/index.html index.html; echo "index mirror:$?"   # must be 0
   cmp -s public/photos.html photos.html; echo "photos mirror:$?" # must be 0
   ```
6. **Back up before editing.** Pattern:
   ```bash
   TS=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
   mkdir -p "backups/<change-slug>-${TS}"
   cp public/index.html "backups/<change-slug>-${TS}/public__index.html"
   cp public/photos.html "backups/<change-slug>-${TS}/public__photos.html"
   cp public/assets/css/site.css "backups/<change-slug>-${TS}/public__assets__css__site.css"
   ```
   The `.vercelignore` already excludes `backups/**` from deploys.
7. **`.vercelignore` is the production gate.** Don't add files to `public/` that aren't ready to ship. Archive / research / qa / backup dirs stay outside `public/`.
8. **Phone obfuscation pattern.** Visible text is plain `910.228.8034`. The `tel:`/`sms:` hrefs use the masked `+191****8034` and rely on browser URL normalization. Don't "fix" the asterisks without asking — see F1 in the June 9 audit. If you do refactor, do it as a `data-tel` + JS rewrite, not by hardcoding the real number into the HTML.
9. **Logo files.** Approved logo is `vera-roofing-logo-mainmark-transparent-clean.png` (purple/silver/white, "Logo 10"). Don't restore the older rejected logo versions in `vector-logo-work/` unless Alex explicitly asks. Don't restore the utility knife or circled-hammer decorations.
10. **Don't drag in old archives.** The `archive/preview-choice-board-2026-06-02/` folder is done — those 5 design directions were reviewed and the live site is the winner. The 30+ subdirs under `qa/` are historical captures. `macbook/` is the Mac offload archive. Reference only.

## 9. Recommended sequence for the new session

1. **Read this file end-to-end.** Then read:
   - `00-NEXT-SESSION-HANDOFF.md` (historical context)
   - `README.md` and `EDITING-GUIDE.md` (maintenance docs)
   - `qa/live-snapshot-20260609-205531/AUDIT.md` (the most recent live audit)
   - `qa/phase2-punch-list-20260604.md` (P1 mobile issues, may or may not be resolved)
   - `public/index.html`, `public/photos.html`, `public/assets/css/site.css` (the source)

2. **Run the mirror check** (§7 #5). Confirm `cmp -s` returns 0.

3. **Run a fresh live browser pass** to confirm the site still matches the June 9 audit:
   - Use `browser_navigate` to `https://vera-roofing-review-public.vercel.app/`
   - `browser_console` (clear first)
   - `browser_snapshot` for structure
   - Visually inspect via vision tool
   - Repeat for `/photos.html`

4. **Tackle O3 (Hyperstock)** — easiest win, no Alex input needed if you go with a defensible replacement. Suggested replacements (pick one, ask Alex):
   - "Low-slope & specialty membranes (TPO, modified bitumen, EPDM)"
   - "Low-slope & flat-roof systems (EPDM, TPO, modified bitumen)"
   - "Low-slope membranes and specialty roof systems"
   Apply to all 3 occurrences in `public/index.html` + mirror to root. Add `qa/HYPERSTOCK-FIX.md` note. Backup first.

5. **Tackle O4 (sitemap)** — 2-line edit to `public/sitemap.xml`, mirror to root (if applicable, but sitemap is only in `public/`). Update `<lastmod>` to today's date or the next real deploy date.

6. **Tackle O2 (reword)** — find every "owner inspects" line, propose 2-3 rewrites, ask Alex which he prefers, apply. Backup first.

7. **Tackle O1 (EPDM photos)** — coordinate with Alex on getting the originals off the iPhone. See §5.

8. **Verify P1 mobile fixes (O5)** — the June 4 punch list said P1 was still open. The June 5 deploy widened the logo, but the underlying overflow may or may not have been fixed. Use `browser_navigate` at narrow viewport (browser tools support viewport resize — check `browser_console` for `window.innerWidth` setting or scroll for `page.setViewport`). Don't claim fixed without a fresh capture.

9. **Optional polish: O6, O7.** Only if Alex asks.

## 10. Tools and gotchas specific to this Windows laptop

- **Hermes local browser = real local Chromium, real viewport, headed.** This is the default. Don't reach for headless Edge or Node CDP unless Alex explicitly asks.
- **Node CDP hangs in this environment.** Confirmed twice (June 4, June 9). The `qa/scripts/capture-fullpage-qa.mjs` script that uses Edge `--remote-debugging-port=9333` + CDP and the patched `capture-live-qa-chrome.mjs` both hang indefinitely on `Page.navigate` after `Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride`. Don't try to fix this in-session — use the browser tool instead. (The June 4 phase 2 captures still in `qa/phase2-fullpage-20260604T214727Z/` are the most recent valid full-page screenshots.)
- **Edge `--screenshot` flag is viewport-only**, not full-page. Good enough for spot-checks. Use `qa/visual/` as the most recent viewport spot-checks.
- **Python: `python3` is missing, `python=3.11.15` exists.** `uv` is installed. Use `python` or `uv run python` for scripts.
- **No git in this workspace.** `git status` will fail. The `backups/` directory is the version history.
- **Vercel CLI**: assume it's installed (Vercel has been deployed to from this laptop before, per the June 4 and June 5 deploy verifications in `qa/deploy-verification-20260604.md`). Don't run it without explicit approval.
- **Apple Photos / iCloud**: see §5. No automatic path. Need human help.
- **No live VPS.** `moltbot-vps` may still be up but is no longer the source of truth. Verify with `ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 ubuntu@moltbot-vps echo ok` before relying on it. The old `clients/gustavo-vera-roofing/` and `staging/vera-roofing-proposals/` dirs on the VPS are historical — the live site is the synthesis, not any VPS prototype.
- **Voice messages common.** If a message has no text or partial text, treat it as genuine and act on substance. Don't be pedantic about "you didn't say X explicitly."

## 12. Verification commands (run at the start and end of every session)

```bash
cd '/c/Users/alexl/Documents/Codex/LarryBuildsAI/local-only/vera-roofing-recovery/deploy-public/vera-roofing-review-public'

# Source-of-truth check
cmp -s public/index.html index.html; echo "index mirror:$?"   # must be 0
cmp -s public/photos.html photos.html; echo "photos mirror:$?" # must be 0

# File count check (should be 26 images + 2 logos + 1 social card)
ls public/assets/images/ | wc -l

# .vercelignore is intact and includes the handoff exclusion
grep -E "^(backups|qa|research|archive|mcps|terminals|00-NEXT-SESSION-HANDOFF|HANDOFF-)" .vercelignore

# Forbidden claim scan
grep -iE "guaranteed insurance approval|bbb accredited|gaf certified|fortified certified|family owned since|best roofer|top roofer" public/index.html public/photos.html

# Hyperstock scan (should be 0 hits after O3 fix)
grep -i "hyperstock" public/index.html public/photos.html

# Owner-inspects / contradiction scan (should reduce after O2)
grep -i "owner inspects" public/index.html public/photos.html

# Live site is reachable
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code} %{url_effective}\n' https://vera-roofing-review-public.vercel.app/
```
